meta_title: For Students: design with AI to Solve can't debug code (Intermediate Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to design with AI and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Design with AI to Fix Debugging Struggles
content.seo_description: Master AI-assisted debugging. Learn to use Antigravity and Claude Code to turn complex coding errors into portfolio-ready projects as a student.
content.answer_block: Debugging isn't just about fixing lines; it's about architectural clarity. By leveraging AI-driven design patterns, you shift from reactive patching to proactive development. Use Antigravity to visualize logic flows and Claude Code to automate remediation, turning your most frustrating bugs into learning milestones that define your engineering career.
content.intro:
The screen flickers with a wall of red text—a stack trace that feels like a dead end. You’ve been staring at the same function for three hours, and the logic that made sense at 2:00 AM now feels like a labyrinth. This is the crucible of every great engineer: the moment where frustration meets the potential for a breakthrough.

Stop fighting the syntax and start designing the solution. By integrating AI into your development workflow, you stop being a frantic debugger and start being a system architect. With tools like Antigravity and Claude Code, you can bridge the gap between intent and execution, transforming your messy code into production-grade software.

steps.title: Visualize the Logic Flow
steps.description: Before fixing the bug, map out the intended state of your application to identify where the logic diverges.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current file structure and generate a Mermaid diagram of the data flow, highlighting potential bottlenecks or state conflicts.
steps.claude_code_command: claude read ./src/logic.ts --ask 'Generate a state diagram for this function'

steps.title: Isolate the Error Surface
steps.description: Use AI to scope the bug, ensuring you aren't chasing ghosts in unrelated modules.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Identify the specific line and dependency chain causing the runtime error in the provided stack trace.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --run 'npm test' --ask 'Identify the root cause of this failure'

steps.title: Refactor for Testability
steps.description: Rewrite the problematic function into smaller, testable units to make the bug obvious.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor this monolithic function into pure, testable units following the Single Responsibility Principle.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit ./src/buggy_module.ts --instruction 'Modularize this function for better unit testing'

steps.title: Automate Validation
steps.description: Create a test suite that ensures the bug never returns by codifying the fix.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a robust suite of unit tests for this module to cover edge cases and prevent regression.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add ./tests/new_fix.test.ts --instruction 'Write tests for the refactored logic'

steps.title: Architectural Documentation
steps.description: Document the fix so it becomes a learning asset for your portfolio.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a README.md snippet explaining the architectural change made to fix this bug.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit ./README.md --instruction 'Add a technical post-mortem for the recent bug fix'

pro_tips: Treat AI as a junior pair programmer, not a magic wand; always verify the logic before committing.
Use Claude Code to create a 'context dump' before every major refactor to save your progress state.
Focus on 'Error-Driven Development'—use the error message as the prompt for your architectural design.
Always ask the AI to explain the 'why' behind a fix to ensure you actually learn the underlying concept.
Keep your context window clean by deleting temporary test files generated during the debugging process.
content.next_steps: Once you have successfully debugged your project, push your code to GitHub, write a brief blog post about the architectural shift you performed, and start building your next feature.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting bugs stall your progress. Master the workflow that turns code errors into career-defining portfolio pieces. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.

Use AI to scope the bug, ensuring you aren't chasing ghosts in unrelated modules.
Rewrite the problematic function into smaller, testable units to make the bug obvious.
Create a test suite that ensures the bug never returns by codifying the fix.
Document the fix so it becomes a learning asset for your portfolio.
